summary: Spolsky proposes a simple but practical product idea: a desk-mountable power strip that combines electrical outlets, a network hub, and a USB hub in one unit. He notes that an existing product by Mockett comes close but requires users to hardwire their own ports rather than offering built-in connectivity. The core frustration is having to crawl under desks to plug in laptops and peripherals.
key_points: The best product ideas often come from everyday frustrations like crawling under desks to plug things in
Combining existing technologies (power strip + network hub + USB hub) into one integrated product can solve real workflow problems
Products that come 'tantalizingly close' to solving a problem but require DIY assembly miss the point of convenience
money_quotes: This would make it easy to plug in laptops, USB peripherals, and all your rechargers at your desk without crawling around on the floor.
key_insight: Great product opportunities exist in combining existing technologies into integrated solutions that eliminate everyday annoyances.
